user_query:
I'm a devoted Joan Rivers fan and I keep a personal "laugh log" of stand-up bits I watch. Please set up a small, locally runnable pipeline that I can schedule for weekday mornings to generate a quick digest. Use the CSV at input/laugh_log.csv as the only data source and do not use the internet. Deliverables and behavior:

1) Scheduling setup
- Create schedule/crontab.txt containing a single cron entry that runs every weekday (Mon–Fri) at 09:00 local time and executes the full pipeline. The command it schedules must: (a) run the processor, then (b) run validations. Concretely, the scheduled command should invoke both of the scripts you create below in sequence so that validation runs right after processing.
- Also provide a convenience script scripts/run_now.sh that runs the exact same command chain once on demand (so I can test it immediately without installing the cron entry).

2) Processing
- Implement scripts/process_laugh_log.py that reads input/laugh_log.csv and writes all outputs into output/.
- Cleaning rule: treat a record as valid only if laugh_score is an integer in [1,10] and duration_seconds > 0. Use only valid records for the outputs below.
- Filtering/sorting/ranking (essential): from the valid records, produce output/top_joan_rivers.csv containing the top 5 Joan Rivers bits with laugh_score >= 6, sorted by: (1) laugh_score desc, then (2) date desc, then (3) bit_title asc. Include columns: id,date,comedian,bit_title,tag,laugh_score,duration_seconds,source.
- Statistics/aggregates (essential): generate output/stats.json with keys:
  - total_records (all rows in input)
  - valid_records
  - joan_records_valid
  - joan_avg_laugh_score_valid (mean of laugh_score for Joan Rivers over valid records)
  - avg_laugh_score_by_comedian_valid (object mapping comedian -> average laugh_score over valid records)
  - top_comedians_by_avg_valid (list of the top 3 comedians ranked by average laugh_score over valid records, only including comedians with at least 2 valid records; break ties by comedian name asc). Store numeric averages as floats (any reasonable precision is fine).
- Write output/last_run.txt that includes an ISO timestamp of the run and a one-line summary of how many valid records were processed.

3) Tests/validation (essential)
- Implement scripts/validate_outputs.py that recomputes expected values from input/laugh_log.csv and verifies:
  (a) output/top_joan_rivers.csv contains only Joan Rivers rows and is sorted/ranked exactly by the rule above; 
  (b) stats in output/stats.json match recomputed values from the same cleaning rules; 
  (c) top_joan_rivers.csv has at most 5 rows.
- The validator must write output/validation_report.json with {"status": "ok"} when all checks pass and include a brief list of checks performed; if any check fails, it should write {"status": "fail", ...details} and exit non-zero. The scheduled command (and scripts/run_now.sh) should run the validator after processing.

Paths to use:
- Input: input/laugh_log.csv (provided)
- Outputs (must be created by your code):
  - output/top_joan_rivers.csv
  - output/stats.json
  - output/validation_report.json
  - output/last_run.txt
- Scheduling artifacts:
  - schedule/crontab.txt (one cron line for 09:00 Mon–Fri that runs processor then validator)
  - scripts/run_now.sh (runs the same command chain once)

Make sure the filter, sort/rank, and statistics steps are core to the pipeline (not optional). I'm after a dependable weekday morning summary celebrating Joan Rivers’ funniest bits, with a built-in validation step to keep me honest. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
